reported_entities.name: Apple
reported_entities.claim: Apple is TSMC's largest customer, projected at 22-25% of 2026 revenue share; historically ~23% of TSMC revenue; over 50% of early 2nm allocation for 2026-2027.

reported_entities.name: NVIDIA
reported_entities.claim: NVIDIA is a top TSMC customer (~11% of 2026 projected revenue share); H100 built on TSMC's N4 node; reserved 2nm capacity; has at times overtaken Apple as top customer by revenue.

reported_entities.name: Broadcom
reported_entities.claim: Broadcom sources 75% of its wafers from TSMC for wireless, networking, broadband and storage products; projected 11-15% of TSMC's 2026 revenue and could become TSMC's second-largest client.

reported_entities.name: MediaTek
reported_entities.claim: MediaTek is among the first customers for TSMC's 2nm node; projected 9-10% of TSMC's 2026 revenue share.

reported_entities.name: Qualcomm
reported_entities.claim: Qualcomm became one of TSMC's largest customers, driven by N6/N5/N4-based chips and automotive/IoT exposure; projected ~8% of TSMC's 2026 revenue; reserved 2nm capacity.

reported_entities.name: AMD
reported_entities.claim: AMD is among the first customers for TSMC's 2nm node; projected ~7% of TSMC's 2026 revenue share; reserved 2nm capacity.

reported_entities.name: Intel
reported_entities.claim: Intel is listed among TSMC's top-10 customers by revenue, outsourcing some chip production to TSMC; projected ~7% of TSMC's 2026 revenue share (though absent from initial 2nm customer list).

reported_entities.name: Marvell
reported_entities.claim: Marvell is listed among TSMC's top-10 customers by revenue.

reported_entities.name: NXP Semiconductors
reported_entities.claim: NXP is listed among TSMC's top-10 customers by revenue.

reported_entities.name: Unisoc
reported_entities.claim: Unisoc is listed among TSMC's top-10 customers by revenue.

reported_entities.name: Google
reported_entities.claim: Google's Tensor G5 chip (Pixel 10) is manufactured by TSMC on N3P process, marking Google's switch from Samsung to TSMC as its fully custom SoC fab partner.

reported_entities.name: Amazon (AWS)
reported_entities.claim: Amazon's AWS Trainium AI chips are produced using TSMC's 3nm process and CoWoS advanced packaging; AWS CoWoS wafer usage expected to rise from 5,000 (2025) to 70,000 wafers in 2026.

reported_entities.name: Tesla
reported_entities.claim: Tesla is using TSMC's N3P process variant for its next-generation HW5 Full-Self-Driving (FSD) chip.

reported_entities.name: Sony
reported_entities.claim: Sony is a TSMC customer/partner, notably co-investing in TSMC's Kumamoto, Japan fab (JASM joint venture) alongside Denso.

notes: Used 3 WebSearch queries (within 5-query budget) via bare web search, no Bash. Companies compiled from multiple corroborating sources: TSMC's disclosed top-10 customer list (Apple, Qualcomm, AMD, Broadcom, NVIDIA, MediaTek, Intel, Marvell, NXP, Unisoc) plus well-documented named customers for specific process nodes/products (Google Tensor G5 on N3P, Amazon AWS Trainium on N3/CoWoS, Tesla HW5 on N3P, Sony via JASM Kumamoto JV). Apple, NVIDIA, Broadcom, MediaTek, Qualcomm, AMD, and Intel are the most heavily documented by revenue share; Google/Amazon/Tesla/Sony are documented via specific chip/fab partnership reporting rather than revenue-share breakdowns.
